subject: Statistics
title: Double-Blind Design
description: A micro-lesson that makes Double-Blind Design usable.

hook.text: If the doctor knows who got the real drug, they unconsciously treat that patient differently.

buildup.text: In a double-blind trial, neither patients nor doctors know who received treatment or placebo.

discovery.text: This prevents bias in reporting symptoms, measuring outcomes, and deciding when to intervene.

twist.text: Blinding is sometimes impossible. You can't fake a surgery or hide a prosthetic limb.

climax.text: When blinding breaks — through side effects or taste — the trial's integrity is compromised.

punchline.text: Nobody knows who got what. That's the only way to be sure.

quiz.question: What does 'double-blind' mean in a clinical trial?
options: Neither patients nor researchers know who received the treatment or placebo
Two separate trials are run simultaneously
Patients are tested twice with different drugs
